consensus_findings.description: src/assistant/core/graph_client.py:_full_url rejects raw '..' segments but not URL-encoded '%2e%2e'. Extensions URL-encode via urllib.parse.quote(safe='') before interpolation, so user-controlled IDs cannot slip '..' through. Internal callers use literal path constants; risk surface is bounded to developer error rather than user input.

src/assistant/core/graph_client.py:max_retry_after_seconds is a GraphClient constructor kwarg but personas configure their extensions through YAML; there is no current path for a persona to override the cap from configuration. The default 60.0 matches prior hardcoded behavior so existing personas are unaffected, but the new knob is effectively code-only.

src/assistant/extensions/outlook.py + teams.py + sharepoint.py: _validate_path_segment is now a shared name across all four real extensions but signatures differ (outlook: value first with kwarg parameter; teams: name first then value; sharepoint: name first then value, no encoding return). The outlook docstring flags this asymmetry but a future engineer grepping for the function across extensions may not notice the signature differences immediately.

src/assistant/extensions/outlook.py:_send_email now raises ValueError on empty 'to' list (added in iteration 1 fix EXT-1). No test exercises this edge case — the existing send_email tests all pass non-empty lists.

src/assistant/harnesses/sdk/ms_agent_fw.py:_agent_framework_install_error returns RuntimeError. Python convention is that import-related failures raise ImportError; RuntimeError reads as 'you cannot run this code right now' rather than 'you cannot import this module'. Tests exercise the path and pass.

P9 retries do not increment trace_graph_call retry_attempt: src/assistant/core/graph_client.py:489-638 defaults retry_attempt to 0, while wrappers at src/assistant/core/graph_client.py:722, src/assistant/core/graph_client.py:757, and src/assistant/core/graph_client.py:892 re-invoke through @resilient_http without passing the actual P9 attempt number, so a 503-then-200 retry emits spans with retry_attempt=0 twice, violating the observability spec and D15.

get_bytes bypasses the 401 invalid_token refresh path: src/assistant/core/graph_client.py:940-991 acquires one token, streams once, and raises GraphAPIError for non-success responses without checking WWW-Authenticate or calling acquire_token(force_refresh=True), violating the graph-client Authentication Token Refresh on 401 requirement for binary downloads.

retry_safe=False POSTs can leave the graph breaker stuck half-open: src/assistant/core/graph_client.py:780-807 records failures for non-retrying POSTs but never records success, and CircuitBreaker.acquire_admission only clears the probe flag in src/assistant/core/resilience.py:271-279, so a successful half-open send_email/post_chat_message leaves state='half_open' and future calls short-circuit.

teams.post_chat_message does not match the spec contract: src/assistant/extensions/teams.py:227-238 exposes a content argument, but the spec scenario calls text='hello'; src/assistant/extensions/teams.py:401-425 also sends {'body': {'content': content, 'contentType': 'text'}} while the spec requires exactly {'body': {'content': 'hello'}}.

MSAgentFrameworkHarness does not consume ContextProvider despite D10/Capability Consumption: src/assistant/harnesses/sdk/ms_agent_fw.py:155-175 calls compose_system_prompt directly and the harness constructor has no context_provider injection path, so custom CapabilityResolver context providers are ignored.

Timeout errors are not consistently mapped to GraphAPIError as required: src/assistant/core/graph_client.py:506-525 catches only httpx.ReadTimeout, so ConnectTimeout, WriteTimeout, and PoolTimeout propagate as raw httpx exceptions; get_bytes at src/assistant/core/graph_client.py:957-1037 has no timeout-to-GraphAPIError mapping at all.

src/assistant/core/graph_client.py:932: GraphClient.get_bytes lacks the 401 invalid_token refresh logic. While GET/POST use _send_with_auth_retry (D9), get_bytes uses client.stream directly and raises on 401 without attempting exactly one force_refresh, violating the 'Authentication Token Refresh on 401' requirement in graph-client/spec.md.

src/assistant/core/graph_client.py:366,932: GraphClient.get_bytes fails to follow redirects required for SharePoint content. SharePoint document downloads (/content) typically return 302/307 redirects to pre-signed URLs. With follow_redirects=False (D4) and no manual redirect handling in get_bytes, download_document will fail with GraphAPIError on the 3xx response.

src/assistant/core/graph_client.py:821: GraphClient._post_no_retry does not call record_success() on successful completion. This prevents non-idempotent tool calls (like outlook.send_email) from closing a 'half_open' breaker, even when they succeed, delaying recovery until a 'retry_safe=True' call occurs.

src/assistant/core/graph_client.py:512: _send_with_auth_retry only catches httpx.ReadTimeout to emit spans. Other transport exceptions (ConnectError, ConnectTimeout, etc.) retried by @resilient_http will not emit a high-fidelity trace_graph_call span for that attempt, leading to blind spots in transport-tier telemetry.

src/assistant/extensions/ms_graph.py:321: MsGraphExtension.create_extension factory signature is missing the 'client' kwarg. Unlike outlook, teams, and sharepoint extensions, the ms_graph factory cannot be called with an injected MockGraphClient, causing inconsistency in the test-layer interface (D26).
